name: Baladeva Vidyabhushana
dates_notes: Late Mughal / early colonial; dates well-established by manuscript and inscriptional record.
core_thesis:
Baladeva Vidyābhūṣaṇa is the formal Vedāntist of the Gauḍīya school. The Six Gosvāmīs' *acintya-bhedābheda* is preserved without doctrinal alteration — Kṛṣṇa as *svayaṃ-bhagavān* (*Govinda-Bhāṣya* on *Brahma-Sūtra* 1.1.1, anchored on *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* 1.3.28), the threefold *śakti* with *acintya-śakti* as load-bearing concept (*Govinda-Bhāṣya* on *Brahma-Sūtra* 2.1.27, *śabda-mūlatvāt*; *Prameya-Ratnāvalī* 7), the *jīva* as eternal *taṭastha-aṃśa* (*Govinda-Bhāṣya* on *Brahma-Sūtra* 2.3.43, *aṃśo nānā-vyapadeśād*; *Prameya-Ratnāvalī* 6), *bhakti* and *prema* as *abhidheya* and *prayojana* (*Prameya-Ratnāvalī* 4–5). The contribution is the institutional achievement the school had previously lacked: a complete Brahma-Sūtra commentary in classical Vedāntic form.

The doctrinal framing draws openly on the Madhva *paramparā*. Baladeva was trained in Mādhva Vedānta before his Gauḍīya commitment, and the *Govinda-Bhāṣya* accordingly reads with a Dvaita-inflected emphasis on the irreducibility of *bheda* — clearer in places than the Gosvāmīs themselves had pressed it (*Govinda-Bhāṣya* on *Brahma-Sūtra* 2.3.43, retaining Madhva's *Tattva-Saṅkhyāna* construal of *aṃśa* as asymmetric dependence) — while preserving the *acintya-śakti* qualification that distinguishes the Gauḍīya position from straight Madhva-Dvaita (*Prameya-Ratnāvalī* 7, *acintya-śaktyā jīva-brahmaṇor bhedābhedaḥ*). The hybridity is structural rather than incidental, and Baladeva makes it explicit by formally embedding the Gauḍīya line in the *brahma-mādhva-gauḍīya paramparā* (*Prameya-Ratnāvalī* 9, *guru-paramparā*).

With the *Prameya-Ratnāvalī* and the *Siddhānta-Ratnam*, the school's doctrinal profile is set out in the standard scholastic forms — *bhāṣya*, *prakaraṇa*, catechetical verse with auto-commentary — by which one is recognised as a legitimate *sampradāya* in late-medieval inter-Vedāntic discourse. The *Govinda-Bhāṣya*'s successful presentation at the eighteenth-century Galta debate and the consequent admission of the Gauḍīyas to the recognised *catur-sampradāya* register the achievement institutionally (the prefatory *maṅgalācaraṇa* of the *Govinda-Bhāṣya* records Govindajī's commission). The transition completed under Baladeva is from a tradition organised around Vraja-experience and *bhakti-rasa* into one that also meets the standard expectations of pan-Indian Vedāntic legitimacy on those expectations' own terms.

engaged_works.title: Govinda-Bhashya
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored formal Gauḍīya Brahma-Sūtra commentary.
engaged_works.summary: The *Govinda-Bhāṣya* is the Gauḍīya school's formal Brahma-Sūtra commentary. Baladeva composed it in eighteenth-century Jaipur in direct response to a challenge from the Rāmānanda-Sampradāya at the Galta court: the Caitanya-Vaiṣṇavas could not be admitted to the company of legitimate *sampradāyas*, the challenge ran, because they lacked a *bhāṣya* on the *Brahma-Sūtras*. The work is therefore both a doctrinal text and a sectarian credential, and the institutional stakes inflect the bhāṣya's organisation. Sectarian tradition reports that Baladeva produced the work rapidly under inspiration from Govindajī himself; the story is part of the school's self-understanding and explains the title (recorded in the bhāṣya's prefatory *maṅgalācaraṇa*). Doctrinally each *adhikaraṇa* is read through *acintya-bhedābheda*: the threefold *śakti*-doctrine (*Govinda-Bhāṣya* on *Brahma-Sūtra* 2.1.27, deploying *Viṣṇu-Purāṇa* 6.7.61), Kṛṣṇa as *svayaṃ-bhagavān* (*Govinda-Bhāṣya* on 1.1.1, anchored on *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* 1.3.28), the *jīva* as *taṭastha-aṃśa* (*Govinda-Bhāṣya* on 2.3.43, *aṃśo nānā-vyapadeśād*), and *bhakti–prema* as the operative path (*Govinda-Bhāṣya* on 3.4 *adhyāya*, the *sādhana*-discussion). The Madhva-inflected emphasis on the irreducibility of *bheda* gives the work its characteristic flavour and distinguishes it from the Gosvāmī systematic theology that more often emphasised the *acintya* qualifier as such (compare *Govinda-Bhāṣya* on 2.3.43 with Jīva's softer treatment at *Paramātma-Sandarbha* §28). The successful presentation at Galta secured Gauḍīya admission to the recognised *catur-sampradāya* lineage and is therefore an institutional milestone in the school's history as much as a doctrinal text.

engaged_works.title: Siddhanta-Ratnam
engaged_works.summary: The *Siddhānta-Ratnam*, sometimes circulated alongside the *Vaiṣṇava-Nandinī* tradition's doctrinal material, is Baladeva's compendium of settled Gauḍīya philosophical positions in the manner of a *prameya-saṅgraha*. The topics on which any classical Vedānta school is expected to take a stance are stated, defended, and demarcated against the rival schools — Advaita, Viśiṣṭādvaita, Madhva-Dvaita, Nimbārka, and the *bhedābheda* lines. The catalogue is the standard one (treated successively across the work's chapters): *pramāṇa*-theory (*Siddhānta-Ratnam* 1, on the threefold *pratyakṣa-anumāna-śabda* with *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* as *pramāṇa-śiromaṇi*), the nature of Brahman as Kṛṣṇa (*Siddhānta-Ratnam* 2), the *jīva* as *taṭastha-aṃśa* (*Siddhānta-Ratnam* 3), the *jagat* as real *pariṇāma* through *māyā-śakti* (*Siddhānta-Ratnam* 4), the Brahman–*jīva*–*jagat* relation under *acintya-śakti* (*Siddhānta-Ratnam* 5), *bandha* and *mokṣa* (*Siddhānta-Ratnam* 6), the *upāya* of *bhakti* and the *upeya* of *prema* (*Siddhānta-Ratnam* 7). The work's structural function complements the bhāṣya. Where the *Govinda-Bhāṣya* is constrained by *sūtra*-form and proceeds *adhikaraṇa* by *adhikaraṇa*, the *Siddhānta-Ratnam* permits Baladeva to set out *acintya-bhedābheda* synoptically and polemically, with the relevant rival positions in view at each step. It functions as the school's portable doctrinal reference and as the systematic supplement to the bhāṣya.

engaged_works.title: Prameya-Ratnavali
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored summary of Gauḍīya prameyas.
engaged_works.summary: The *Prameya-Ratnāvalī* ("jewelled necklace of established truths") is Baladeva's tightly compressed catechetical summary of the nine Gauḍīya *prameyas*. The list is canonical: Kṛṣṇa as *svayaṃ-bhagavān* (*Prameya-Ratnāvalī* 1, anchored on *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* 1.3.28); his being known by all the *Vedas* (*Prameya-Ratnāvalī* 2); the *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* as the supreme *pramāṇa* (*Prameya-Ratnāvalī* 3); *bhakti* alone as *abhidheya* (*Prameya-Ratnāvalī* 4); *prema* as *prayojana* (*Prameya-Ratnāvalī* 5); the *jīva*'s natural condition as eternal servant of Kṛṣṇa (*Prameya-Ratnāvalī* 6); the simultaneous *bheda* and *abheda* of *jīva* and Brahman by *acintya-śakti* (*Prameya-Ratnāvalī* 7); the world as Bhagavān's real *pariṇāma* through *māyā-śakti* (*Prameya-Ratnāvalī* 8); the *guru-paramparā* (*Prameya-Ratnāvalī* 9). Each *prameya* is stated in clear verse with auto-commentary, in a form suitable for memorisation and instruction. The lineage-claim is explicit: Baladeva identifies the school with Madhva and embeds the Gauḍīyas within the *brahma-mādhva-gauḍīya paramparā* as he understands it (*Prameya-Ratnāvalī* 9). The connection is doctrinally selective rather than wholesale; what Baladeva borrows from Madhva is the formal Vedāntic apparatus and the strong reading of *bheda* (in the manner of *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya* 1.1.2), not the rejection of *acintya-śakti*. With the *Govinda-Bhāṣya*, the *Prameya-Ratnāvalī* establishes the standard Gauḍīya doctrinal profile as it is presented in inter-sectarian Vedāntic discourse.
